---
title: "Question 3 - One-way ANOVA (Training programs)"
author: "Upayan Mazumder"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
alpha <- 0.05
```

## Question

Compare the performance (tasks per minute) of trainees under four training programs using one-way ANOVA.

```{r data}
program_data <- list(
  Program1 = c(9, 12, 14, 11, 13),
  Program2 = c(10, 6, 9, 9, 10),
  Program3 = c(12, 14, 11, 13, 11),
  Program4 = c(9, 8, 11, 7, 8)
)

df <- stack(program_data)
names(df) <- c("Score", "Program")
df$Program <- factor(df$Program)

df
aggregate(Score ~ Program, data = df, FUN = mean)
```

## One-way ANOVA

```{r anova}
fit <- aov(Score ~ Program, data = df)
anova_table <- summary(fit)
anova_table
```

```{r posthoc}
TukeyHSD(fit)
```

```{r decision}
p_program <- anova_table[[1]]["Program", "Pr(>F)"]

if (p_program < alpha) {
  cat("Decision: Reject H0 at 5% significance level.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: At least one training program has a different mean performance.")
} else {
  cat("Decision: Fail to reject H0 at 5% significance level.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: No significant difference in mean performance among programs.")
}
```
